playbooks.title: Fundraising Trigger
playbooks.subtitle: Series C preparation pipeline
playbooks.description: Structured path from ARR milestone to Series C close. Each step has a data gate — no gut feelings.

costOfInaction.description: Delayed Series C = $42K/mo in valuation gap growth
costOfInaction.basis: ARR growth vs static valuation multiple erosion

steps.title: Fix burn multiple below 2×
steps.description: Reduce SMB acquisition spend. Focus on enterprise where LTV:CAC is 6.2× vs SMB's 2.8×

recommendations.label: Cut SMB acquisition budget 30%
recommendations.detail: SMB CAC payback is 22mo vs Enterprise's 8mo. Redirect $126K/yr to enterprise channel.

recommendations.label: Shift 2 SDRs from SMB to Enterprise
recommendations.detail: Enterprise pipeline coverage improves from 2.8× to 3.4×. SMB pipeline already over-served.

recommendations.label: Raise SMB pricing 20%
recommendations.impact: Burn multiple drops to 1.9×, some churn risk
recommendations.detail: SMB NRR is 96% (below breakeven). Price-sensitive segment, expect 8-12% churn spike.

playbooks.title: Hiring Trigger
playbooks.subtitle: Sales team expansion gate
playbooks.description: Data-gated hiring. Pipeline coverage, rep productivity, and CFO scenario check must all pass before offers go out.

steps.title: CFO scenario check
steps.description: Bear scenario must show ≥12mo runway with planned hires factored in.
steps.blockReason: Bear scenario shows 11.8mo runway with Plan hiring. Below 12mo minimum.

resolutionOptions.label: Defer hiring 8 weeks
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Pipeline coverage may drop to 2.6× without new reps

resolutionOptions.label: Hire 2 instead of 3
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Slower revenue ramp, ~$180K/yr less new ARR capacity

resolutionOptions.label: Cut marketing spend 15%
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Top-of-funnel lead volume drops ~20% in 2 months

playbooks.title: Pivot / Strategic Direction
playbooks.subtitle: Go/no-go on changing the growth engine
playbooks.description: When core growth metrics plateau for 2+ quarters despite execution, evaluate whether the current strategy can reach the next milestone. This playbook gates the pivot decision with data, not panic.

costOfInaction.description: Continuing a stalled strategy burns $85K/mo in misallocated capital
costOfInaction.basis: SMB segment drag + below-target growth rate = value destruction

steps.title: Growth rate below target for 2+ quarters
steps.completedText: Growth on track, no pivot trigger
steps.description: If net new ARR stays below $1.5M for 2 quarters while competitors grow 20%+, the current GTM engine may be exhausted.

steps.title: Segment health audit
steps.description: Deep dive each segment: enterprise, mid-market, SMB. Which is growing? Which is dragging? Where's PMF strongest?

recommendations.label: Double down on Enterprise
recommendations.detail: Enterprise has 112% NRR, 6.2× LTV:CAC, and $7.2M ARR. This is the growth engine.

recommendations.label: Exit SMB entirely
recommendations.detail: SMB NRR is 96% (below breakeven), $0.52K ARPU declining 5%/quarter. This segment is destroying value.

recommendations.label: Pivot SMB to self-serve PLG
recommendations.impact: Automate onboarding, cut CSM cost to zero
recommendations.detail: Replace human-touch SMB with product-led growth. ARPU drops to $0.3K but cost-to-serve drops 90%.

steps.title: Competitive position assessment
steps.description: Are we losing deals because of features, pricing, or market fit? Win/loss analysis + competitor benchmarking.

steps.title: Board alignment on direction
steps.description: Present the data, the options, and a recommendation. Board must approve any strategic pivot before execution begins.

steps.title: Execute new strategy
steps.description: 90-day execution plan with weekly milestones. First milestone must show traction within 30 days or re-evaluate.
